Long Description: The VDRL (Venereal Disease Research Laboratory) test is one of the traditional screening tests for syphilis, working on a similar principle to the more commonly used RPR test today. A positive result is typically followed by a confirmatory treponemal antibody test to reduce the chance of a false positive. It's used to help screen for syphilis infection and, in patients with known past infection, to help monitor treatment response.

Short Description: Antibody test (thyroid-stimulating immunoglobulin) that helps diagnose Graves' disease, an autoimmune cause of overactive thyroid.
Long Description: Thyroid-stimulating immunoglobulin (TSI) is an antibody that mimics TSH, stimulating the thyroid gland to overproduce hormone — the underlying cause of Graves' disease, the most common cause of hyperthyroidism. This test is used to help confirm a Graves' disease diagnosis, particularly when the cause of hyperthyroidism isn't clear from other testing, and can help predict the likelihood of relapse or guide treatment decisions.

Short Description: Measures vitamin B12 levels, with automatic follow-up testing if results are borderline or suggest further evaluation is needed.
Long Description: Vitamin B12 is essential for red blood cell production and nervous system health. This version includes reflex testing, meaning if the result falls in a borderline range, additional related testing (such as methylmalonic acid, a more sensitive marker of B12 status at the cellular level) is automatically performed to help clarify whether a true deficiency is present.

Short Description: Measures total LDH, with automatic follow-up testing (such as LDH isoenzymes) if results are abnormal.
Long Description: Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) is a nonspecific marker of cell or tissue damage found in many organs. This version includes reflex testing, meaning if the result is abnormal, additional related testing (such as LDH isoenzymes) is automatically performed to help narrow down the likely source of the elevation.

Short Description: Measures sex hormone binding globulin (SHBG), a protein that carries sex hormones, used to help evaluate hormone-related symptoms.
Long Description: Sex hormone binding globulin (SHBG) binds to testosterone and estrogen in the blood, controlling how much of these hormones is freely available for the body to use. This test is used to help interpret total testosterone or estrogen results more accurately, and to help evaluate conditions like PCOS, low testosterone symptoms in men, or other hormone-related concerns where SHBG levels can significantly affect how much active hormone is actually available.

Patient Prep: Requires 2 separate blood draws, timed per your provider's specific protocol — coordinate timing with your provider.
Short Description: Measures FSH across 2 separately timed samples, used to help average out natural fluctuations for a more reliable result.
Long Description: follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) is released in pulses throughout the day, so a single measurement can sometimes be misleading. This protocol pools or analyzes 2 separately timed blood samples to capture a more representative average level, which can be useful in certain fertility or pituitary function evaluations where a single reading isn't considered reliable enough.

Patient Prep: Requires 3 separate blood draws, timed per your provider's specific protocol — coordinate timing with your provider.
Short Description: Measures FSH across 3 separately timed samples, used to help average out natural fluctuations for a more reliable result.
Long Description: follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) is released in pulses throughout the day, so a single measurement can sometimes be misleading. This protocol pools or analyzes 3 separately timed blood samples to capture a more representative average level, which can be useful in certain fertility or pituitary function evaluations where a single reading isn't considered reliable enough.

Patient Prep: Requires 4 separate blood draws, timed per your provider's specific protocol — coordinate timing with your provider.
Short Description: Measures FSH across 4 separately timed samples, used to help average out natural fluctuations for a more reliable result.
Long Description: follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) is released in pulses throughout the day, so a single measurement can sometimes be misleading. This protocol pools or analyzes 4 separately timed blood samples to capture a more representative average level, which can be useful in certain fertility or pituitary function evaluations where a single reading isn't considered reliable enough.

Patient Prep: Requires 2 separate blood draws, timed per your provider's specific protocol — coordinate timing with your provider.
Short Description: Measures LH across 2 separately timed samples, used to help average out natural fluctuations for a more reliable result.
Long Description: luteinizing hormone (LH) is released in pulses throughout the day, so a single measurement can sometimes be misleading. This protocol pools or analyzes 2 separately timed blood samples to capture a more representative average level, which can be useful in certain fertility or pituitary function evaluations where a single reading isn't considered reliable enough.

Patient Prep: Requires 3 separate blood draws, timed per your provider's specific protocol — coordinate timing with your provider.
Short Description: Measures LH across 3 separately timed samples, used to help average out natural fluctuations for a more reliable result.
Long Description: luteinizing hormone (LH) is released in pulses throughout the day, so a single measurement can sometimes be misleading. This protocol pools or analyzes 3 separately timed blood samples to capture a more representative average level, which can be useful in certain fertility or pituitary function evaluations where a single reading isn't considered reliable enough.

Patient Prep: Requires 4 separate blood draws, timed per your provider's specific protocol — coordinate timing with your provider.
Short Description: Measures LH across 4 separately timed samples, used to help average out natural fluctuations for a more reliable result.
Long Description: luteinizing hormone (LH) is released in pulses throughout the day, so a single measurement can sometimes be misleading. This protocol pools or analyzes 4 separately timed blood samples to capture a more representative average level, which can be useful in certain fertility or pituitary function evaluations where a single reading isn't considered reliable enough.

Patient Prep: No special preparation required, though ejaculation, prostate exams, or certain procedures shortly before the test may be asked to be avoided, since they can temporarily affect results.
Short Description: Measures both free and total PSA together, used to help refine prostate cancer risk assessment when total PSA is borderline.
Long Description: This test measures both total PSA and free PSA (the portion not bound to blood proteins), and calculates the percentage of free PSA. A lower percentage of free PSA is associated with a higher likelihood of prostate cancer, making this combined test useful for helping refine risk assessment and potentially avoid unnecessary biopsies when total PSA alone falls in a borderline range.

Short Description: Antibody test (anti-recoverin) that helps evaluate cancer-associated retinopathy, a rare condition causing vision loss related to certain cancers.
Long Description: Cancer-associated retinopathy (CAR) is a rare condition where the immune system, in response to certain cancers, produces antibodies that also attack the retina, causing vision loss. This test detects anti-recoverin antibodies, one of the antibodies associated with CAR, and is typically ordered by an ophthalmologist or oncologist investigating unexplained vision changes in a patient with, or at risk for, certain cancers (most often small cell lung cancer).

Patient Prep: Fasting is often recommended — follow your provider's instructions.
Short Description: Measures homocysteine, an amino acid used as part of cardiovascular risk assessment.
Long Description: Homocysteine is an amino acid that, at elevated levels, has been associated with increased cardiovascular risk, though its role as an independent risk factor (versus a marker of other underlying issues) is still debated in the medical literature. This test is used alongside other cardiovascular risk factors, and elevated levels can also point to vitamin B12 or folate deficiency, so results are typically considered as part of a broader clinical picture.

Short Description: Combined antibody test that checks immunity levels to both tetanus and diphtheria, sometimes used to help guide booster vaccination decisions.
Long Description: This test measures IgG antibody levels against both tetanus and diphtheria toxoids together, reflecting how much protective immunity a person has from past vaccination against both. It's used in specific clinical situations — such as uncertainty about vaccination history, occupational requirements, or evaluating suspected immune deficiencies — to help a provider decide whether booster vaccines are needed.

Product Name (Source): QST0034388 BASIC METAB PNL W/O CA
Short Description: Basic metabolic panel without calcium, covering electrolytes, kidney function, and blood sugar.
Long Description: This is a modified version of the basic metabolic panel (BMP) that excludes calcium, keeping the core electrolyte (sodium, potassium, chloride, CO2) and kidney function (BUN, creatinine) and glucose measurements. It's used the same way as a standard BMP for routine health screening and monitoring, in situations where calcium isn't needed as part of the panel.

Patient Prep: Fasting for 8-12 hours is often recommended, though not always required — follow your provider's instructions.
Short Description: Comprehensive metabolic panel without CO2 and ALT, covering electrolytes, kidney function, blood sugar, and select liver markers.
Long Description: This is a modified version of the comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP) that excludes carbon dioxide (CO2) and ALT, while keeping the other standard electrolyte, kidney function, glucose, and liver-related measurements. It's used the same way as a standard CMP for routine health screening and monitoring, in situations where these two specific components aren't needed as part of the panel.

Product Name (Source): QST0034391 Hepatic Function Panel without Total Protein
Test Name: Hepatic Function Panel without Total Protein
Short Description: Hepatic function panel without total protein, covering liver enzymes, bilirubin, and albumin.
Long Description: This is a modified version of the hepatic (liver) function panel that excludes total protein, while keeping liver enzymes (like ALT and AST), bilirubin, and albumin. It's used the same way as a standard hepatic function panel to help evaluate suspected liver disease or monitor medications known to affect the liver, in situations where total protein isn't needed as part of the panel.

Short Description: Bundled allergy blood test (ImmunoCAP) that checks IgE antibody response to a broad set of common food allergens.
Long Description: This panel groups together several individual ImmunoCAP allergen-specific IgE tests covering a range of foods frequently responsible for food allergy symptoms, rather than testing one allergen at a time. A positive (elevated) result for any allergen in the panel indicates the immune system has produced antibodies against it, which is associated with — but doesn't on its own confirm — a true clinical allergy, so results should be interpreted alongside the patient's symptom history and known exposures. Panels like this are a convenient, often more cost-effective way to screen for multiple related allergens at once rather than ordering each individually; exact allergens included can vary by region/panel version, so it's worth confirming the specific component list with your lab.
